data.title: CB&I to acquire Petrofac’s Asset Solutions business
Lemonade is the best performing property and casualty insurance stock YTD
data.description:
The S&P 500 financials sector has largely kept pace with the broader market in 2025, rising about +14% year to date.

Financials rank as the fourth-best performing sector in the S&P 500 (SP500).

As we look at the sub-sectors within the broader financial sector, below is a list of the top 10 property and casualty insurance stocks ranked by their YTD performance in descending order. Additional data point included is their SA Quant ratings.

The list is topped by Lemonade (LMND), with a YTD performance of +124.97%. HCI Group (HCI) and Hamilton Insurance Group (HG) are next, with YTD performances of +66.39% and +49.08% respectively. Mercury General (MCY) and Palomar Holdings (PLMR) round out the top five.

Several top-performing stocks on this list have earned high Quant ratings, specifically “Strong Buy” designations. These include HCI Group (HCI) with a rating of 4.84, Hamilton Insurance Group (HG) at 4.74, The Hartford Insurance Group (HIG) at 4.69, and Mercury General (MCY) at 4.62.

Here is the list:

  1. Lemonade (LMND), YTD perf: +124.97%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.45)

  2. HCI Group (HCI), YTD perf: +66.39%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.84)

  3. Hamilton Insurance Group (HG), YTD perf: +49.08%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.74)

  4. Mercury General (MCY), YTD perf: +43.32%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.62)

  5. Palomar Holdings (PLMR), YTD perf: +29.43%, Quant Rating: Buy (4.35)

  6. Old Republic International (ORI), YTD perf: +27.63%, Quant Rating: Hold (2.85)

  7. Loews (L), YTD perf: +27.62%

  8. The Hartford Insurance Group (HIG), YTD perf: +27.56%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy (4.69)

  9. Markel Group (MKL), YTD perf: +26.44%, Quant Rating: Buy (4.23)

  10. AXIS Capital Holdings (AXS), YTD perf: +22.95%, Quant Rating: Hold (3.35)

Financials ETFs: (XLF), (VFH), (IYF), (FNCL), (IYG), and (FXO)

data.title: Prudential is the best YTD performer among life and health insurance stocks
data.description:
The S&P 500 financials sector has largely kept pace with the broader market in 2025, rising about +14% year to date.

Financials rank as the fourth-best performing sector in the S&P 500 (SP500).

As we look at the sub-sectors within the broader financial sector, below is a list of the top 10 life and health insurance stocks ranked by their YTD performance. The list includes SA Quant ratings for each stock.

The list is topped by Prudential (PUK), with a YTD performance of +96.80%. Lincoln National (LNC) and Brighthouse Financial (BHF) are next, rounding out the top three performers in the sector.

Among the notable stocks on the list, Aegon (AEG), ranking 5th, holds a Strong Buy Quant Rating of 4.69, making it the highest-rated stock on the list.

data.title: Asia markets muted amid thin trading volumes and BoJ scrutiny
Waymo plans to update software across fleet after San Francisco power outage
data.description:
Waymo, the self-driving car startup owned by Google parent Alphabet (GOOGL), said late on Tuesday that it plans to update its software across the fleet, and update its emergency response protocol, after widespread blackout in the San Francisco Bay Area impacted several of its vehicles over the weekend. 

The disturbance caused by the blackout led Waymo to temporarily halt its service in San Francisco.

Waymo said that as part of fleet-wide updates, it is integrating more information about outages to give its vehicles even more context for them to navigate more decisively. The company said it also plans to improve its emergency response protocols. 

Waymo also plans to expand its first responder engagement, having trained more than 25,000 first responders in the U.S. and around the world on how to interact with its vehicles. 

San Francisco’s widespread power outage over the weekend left several of Waymo’s autonomous vehicles immobilized, snarling traffic across parts of the city.

Videos shared on social media showed Waymo vehicles stopped in traffic lanes or blocking intersections, worsening congestion at a time when power failures had already disabled traffic lights. Drivers reported significant delays as stalled robotaxis added to the gridlock.

data.title: Snowflake eyes app monitoring startup Observe for $1B - report
data.description:
• Snowflake (SNOW) is in advanced talks to acquire Observe—an AI-native observability startup—for approximately $1 billion, The Information reported, citing people familiar with the matter.
  • The potential deal would mark Snowflake's (SNOW) largest acquisition to date amid its push into application monitoring and AI-driven infrastructure tools. The acquisition would bolster Snowflake's capabilities in full-stack observability, accelerating AI workflows for troubleshooting and reducing enterprise costs.
  • Observe, based in San Mateo, specializes in AI-powered observability for enterprise applications, helping developers manage telemetry data at scale using data lake architectures and agentic AI.
  • The two companies already share a strong connection, as Observe utilizes Snowflake’s (SNOW) database technology, people familiar with the data said.

data.title: BoJ unanimously raises rates to 0.75% in year’s second tightening move
Wednesday’s Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM Jobless Claims

New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time.

Claims are expected at 225K, not much changed from 224K last week, which is down 13 from the week before that.

11:00 AM Survey of Business Uncertainty

The survey of business uncertainty is a panel survey measuring year-ahead expectations that US firms have about their own sales and employment growth.

11:30 7-Yr Note Auction

Treasury notes are sold at regularly scheduled public auctions. The competitive bids at these auctions determine the interest rate paid on each Treasury note issue.

NYSE Early Close at 1:00 PM

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs.

SIFMA Early Close at 2:00 PM

data.title: FDA approves Agios’ AQVESME (mitapivat) for the treatment of anemia in adults
data.description:
• Agios Pharmaceuticals (AGIO) said on Tuesday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration has approved AQVESME (mitapivat), an oral pyruvate kinase activator, for the treatment of anemia in adults with alpha- or beta-thalassemia. 
  • With this approved indication, AQVESME becomes the only FDA-approved medicine for anemia in both non-transfusion-dependent and transfusion-dependent alpha- or beta-thalassemia.
  • The FDA approval of AQVESME in thalassemia is based on results from the global, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled ENERGIZE and ENERGIZE-T Phase 3 trials in adults with non-transfusion-dependent and transfusion-dependent alpha- or beta-thalassemia, respectively.
  • Due to the AQVESME REMS program, mitapivat will be marketed under the brand name AQVESME in the U.S. for the thalassemia indication. Mitapivat will continue to be marketed as PYRUKYND (mitapivat) in the U.S. for the PK deficiency indication, which does not require a REMS program. 
  • Outside the U.S., mitapivat will continue to be marketed as PYRUKYND for its PK deficiency and thalassemia indications in regions where it is approved, and will retain this name upon approval in regions currently under regulatory review.
  • Agios expects AQVESME to be available in the U.S. in late January 2026, following implementation of the AQVESME REMS program.

data.title: Lux Metals upsizes private placement to $3.5M
data.description:
• Lux Metals (LXM:CA) said on Tuesday that, further to its news release dated December 10, 2025, due to strong investor demand, it has increased the size of its previously announced private placement from up to 12.5M units of the company to up to 17.5M units at a price of $0.20 per unit for total gross proceeds of up to $3.5M. 
  • Each unit will consist of one common share and one transferable share purchase warrant, each warrant exercisable into one additional share for a period of two years from the date of issue at a price of $0.40 per share. 
  • The gross proceeds from the issuance of the units will be used for exploration costs and general working capital.
